comments.body: github.com/gtiwari333/… check this out. I've implemented SP initiated SSO flow with Spring Boot 3.

@gtiwari333 - your implementation will not fit into the versions I have mentioned. WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter is no longer supported from Spring Security versions 6.0 (minimum spring security version used by Spring Boot version 3.X)..
i've three projects using different versions of spring boot. github.com/gtiwari333/… uses latest spring boot. did you check it out?

I am not sure whether you have read the full question. I need some working examples which will adapt all the functionalities that were there in the Legacy SAML extension project. It would be nice if you provide an answer relevant to it for my migration. Thanks.

answers.body:
Upgrading from the old Spring Security SAML Extension to native SAML2 in Spring Boot 3.x (with Spring Security 6) requires replacing deprecated components with modern auto-configured beans. Spring dropped the legacy SAML extension entirely.

1.Key Replacements:

  • SAMLProcessingFilter → Auto-configured via Saml2LoginConfigurer
  • Manual metadata/key management → RelyingPartyRegistration
  • WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter → SecurityFilterChain bean

2.Minimal Working Example
Add dependency:

<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-security</artifactId>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.springframework.security</groupId>
    <artifactId>spring-security-saml2-service-provider</artifactId>
</dependency>

Configure SAML:

spring:
  security:
    saml2:
      relyingparty:
        registration:
          my-saml-client:
            signing:
              credentials:
                - private-key-location: "classpath:private.key"
                  certificate-location: "classpath:public.crt"
            identityprovider:
              entity-id: "https://your-idp.com/saml2/idp"
              metadata-uri: "https://your-idp.com/saml2/metadata"

Security configuration:

@Configuration
@EnableWebSecurity
public class SecurityConfig {

    @Bean
    SecurityFilterChain samlFilterChain(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception {
        http
            .authorizeHttpRequests(auth -> auth
                .anyRequest().authenticated()
            )
            .saml2Login(Customizer.withDefaults()); // Handles SAML login flow

        return http.build();
    }
}

This mirrors Spring's official SAML2 guide but skips legacy baggage.

title: SAML2 Implementation with Spring Boot Version 3.X and Java 17
body:
I am upgrading the current Spring Security SAML extension project to SAML2 authentication. I found a question where my sample existing security configuration looks like Existing Security Configuration.

There is an official documentation from Spring which is mentioned in the answer. But, I would like to better understand which part of code is getting replaced by which part in the new implementation with some working examples.

Kindly please help. Thanks in advance.
